CPC What skill did you cover.value: The importance of collecting ideas in a sketchbook/portfolio | Found out their backgrounds in the field of art | Reliable work ethic | Researched professional fine artists who use words in or on or titles in their work. | Considered decorative artists and message/storytelling artists, and artshow directors who are also artists. | Building a sketchbook/portfolio | Art colleges require 10-15 portfolio pieces in their application for college. | Using different mediums beside drawing | Physical, salesmanship, inventory for art shows | Reviewed design elements/principles for sketchbook | Planned our portfolio development | portfolio development | Building vocabulary in perspective drawing | Use of color and words in art | Idependent artists Brian Murphy plein air, Jennifer York cut paper sculpture, Melissa Dominiak realism, Michael Madzo collage | Portfolio review | Speaking confidently | Everything is designed | Last week artists presented were Jennifer Ardolino, Barb Umbel, this week Cali Hobgood, Kana Handel, Clifton Henri, Debo Groover                                            o | Portfolio building- value scale and shading objects | Building a resume | portfolio deelopment- color work reviewed | Range of styles to fit your personality/Review perspective-overlappping and diminution/ collage and composiions | Art criticism debates | Art vocabulary | Sketchbook development | portfolio develipment | Principles of Design | perspective tools for portfolio development | portfolio development | art criticism | Portfolio development | portfolio development, review sculptors, begin debates | art criticism

Emphasized the importance of building a sketchbook for a portfolio for art school. | Perspective drawing tools-- overlapping and scale | Reviewed new work in sketchbooks/portfolios. | Art show artists introduced, collage, cut paper, en pein aire, Metallic paint, photo referenced, | Personal strengths and interests in their work | Reviewed career ecosystem, volunteerism and resume building, and portfolios | beginning use of design elements | Values use and simultaneous contrast | Building discussion of artists Darryl Thetford, John Schirmer, Michael Bond | Developing art vocabulary | Other mediums sold by independent artists | Representational form | Art criticism | Talking about artist's work | Using lines for portfolio ideas last week, this week values and shaiding | Using light and shade | Values, shading, and using the whole page, focal point | perspective- overlapping and diminution | artists John Booth, Michael Bond, Michael Madzo, Laura Nugent, Amanda Bennett | Development of vocabulary for art and design | Critiquing art | Using design element-line-introduced/critiqued | Use of Values/gradation | Elements of Design | overlapping, diminution,foremidback and detail perspective | developing your own style | Objective art vocabulary | art criticism | art vocabulary in art criticism in debates | using design elements and principles to discuss art

CPC Discussion topics.value: Described my experiences in school and different careers I considered,  and all careers that would benefit communicating with drawing out ideas to communicate. | Collecting ideas and building a sketchbook/portfolio, described my career path | Reviewed work in their sketchbooks, introduced the work of two very different professional artists who earn a living selling their work. | Will Armstrong, Amanda Bennett, and Michel Delgado artists work and the importance of words in their work. | Considered artists who also organize art shows. | Current artshow artists-- Thomas Wargin, John Schirmer, Michael Bond | Introduced work using color and themes by contemporary artshow artists-- Terrell Powell, Anne Marie Cianciola, Debo Grover, John Booth, Holly Foss, Madison Latimer, and Melissa Dominiak. | Art clubs, mural painting, building a resume. Artists introduced Michael Madzo, Michelle Mardis, Brian Murphy, Kana Handel, Jenn Swearington York. | Described other art related jobs-- graphic design, theatre set design, | Porfolio work and career ecosystem and volunteerism | Described my past studio specialties and what I do now | Introduced the work of Elijah Scarlet and Kate Beck, Clifton Henri | Discussed the work of real independent artists | Artwork being defaced, materials and techniques, using design vocabulary to objectively discuss art | Other mediums sold by independent artists | Evidence of creative thinking for school and job apps | Lively and fun and fulfilling debate of their art | Need to build a portfolio for art career path | Discussed the need for a portfolio for any art related career application. | Melissa Dominiak, Bala Thiagaran, Paul Jeselskis, Jennifer York | Introduced fiber/textile artists, Annie Bisone(video), Kate Beck, Erika Mock, Dawn DeSanto | Contemporary art show artists, John Schirmer, Brian Murphy, William Kwamena poh | Range of styles to fit your personality | Discussed the importance or need to develop concept portfolio in the future, and how to use the art vocbulary in art criticism | Design Elements and Principles in debate of current art | Artists Jennifer Ardonlino and Barbara Umbel, independent professional artists | Reviewed the use of symbols by artists Terrell Powell, Ann Marie Cianciollo, Kana Handel, Debo Groover, | Use of line by artists Jennifer Ardolino, Barbara Umbel | John Schirmer, Jon Booth, Michael Bond, | Critiquing the work of Daryl Thetford and Amanda Bennett-pattern | The work of Annie Bisone, Kate Beck, Erika Mock, Amos Amit | The work of Bala, Holly Foss, JAJA, Reiko and their own work | Reviewed purpose of portfolio, terms for art criticism | We critiqued in debate format best work from the DesMoines Art Festival artists gallelry

CPC Anything else.value:
It took most of the session to understand where they were at developmentally in the field of art. | Eventually I will remember to take a picture of our group!
I must also learn how to access the post-session summary! | Haven't seen my intern Valentina Abreu for awhile. And in your notes of interns, my intern Stacy Stheysis Genao has "Ester" as her nickname, but it should be changed to Stacy. | What a great group of girls! | Loved this session! We accomplish a lot even though we may not use all the requested forms. | This is in some ways an advanced group in terms of their ability to critique art, and I think at the end of the sessions they will have a more clear vision of their own artwork.
